checklistAnswers.question: Is Health-club/Organizational Membership currently approved in the budget?
Does the membership provide opportunity for community inclusion?
Does the membership accommodate the individual's health and/or safety needs?
Does the membership increase independence or substitute for human assistance?
Is the membership provided exclusively toward the benefit of the participant? (not family)
Is the organization open to the public and not a private/invitation-only club?

parsed.formTitle: Health Club / Organizational Memberships Pre-approval Checklist
parsed.valuedOutcome: Miriam will engage in community and cultural activities through an individual museum membership open to the public.

webChecks.label: Organization is open to the public (not private/invitation-only)
webChecks.criteria: Anyone from the general public must be able to join (public 'join'/'sign up' flow, published membership tiers). Private or invitation-only clubs fail.

webChecks.label: Membership fee is published
webChecks.criteria: A concrete membership price (monthly or yearly) must be publicly visible.

webChecks.label: Published membership fee matches the form (yearly/monthly)
webChecks.criteria: Compare the membership fee on the application against the published amount, being careful about monthly vs yearly. Verdict: matches application exactly / differs / not published.

findings.note: The Brooklyn Museum website clearly presents various membership levels with options to "Join or renew", indicating it is open to the public.
findings.label: Organization is open to the public (not private/invitation-only)

findings.note: The website explicitly lists an annual fee of $80 for an Individual Membership.
findings.label: Membership fee is published

findings.note: The website lists an Individual Membership for $80, which matches the requested fee on the form.
findings.label: Published membership fee matches the form (yearly/monthly)

rateComparison.note: The website lists an Individual Membership for $80, which matches the $80.00 stated on the application form.

MEMBERSHIP
PATRON GROUPS
PLANNED GIVING
BECOME A MEMBER

You belong here. Members help make possible the great art, big ideas, and courageous conversations that can only happen at the Brooklyn Museum.

See the benefits
Current Members
DID WE MENTION THE PERKS?
Everyone who comes to the Museum is a VIP. But Members enjoy next-level benefits:
Free admission

Reserve free, untimed tickets to the Museum, including special exhibitions. Just show your digital Membership card (physical card available on request).

Early and exclusive access

You’ll be on the list for exclusive previews, viewing hours, and programs. Get first dibs on tickets to special exhibitions, First Saturdays (skip the line!), the Brooklyn Artists Ball After Party, and more.

Discounts

Save 10% on dining, shopping, tours, and art classes; 20% on your first Shop purchase; 30% on admission for additional guests; and 40% on ticketed programs.

Stay up to date

You’ll receive Members-only emails with upcoming events, opportunities, and Member spotlights.

Reciprocal benefits

Score free admission and discounted purchases at 40+ partner museums when you join at the Enthusiast level and above. Learn more about reciprocal benefits.

Special programs

Enjoy Members-only programming and behind-the-scenes insights at any level, plus additional opportunities to hear from curators, artists, and experts at the Enthusiast level and above.

MEMBERSHIP LEVELS
We’re excited to introduce new levels and benefits for 2025! Already a Member and wondering how this will affect your Membership? Visit the Member Hub for answers.

Adults 65+ and visitors with disabilities can save $10 on annual Individual  and Dual  Memberships.

Join or renew

Become a Groundbreaker

Deepen your engagement and impact while enjoying unique benefits, including select Director’s Circle events. This Membership level now also includes annual invitations to two artist events, such as studio tours or gallery visits, and a 30% discount on admission for additional guests.

Join or renew
Donate to the Annual Fund

Your generous support makes possible the great art, inspiring encounters, and courageous conversations that can only happen here.

Give today
Already a Member?

Welcome back! Log in to manage your Membership  or visit the Member Hub to reserve tickets, see upcoming events, and maximize your perks.

Visit the Member Hub

Join the Kids’ Club

Let your little art enthusiast (ages 12 and under) unlock a world of creativity and hands-on museum adventures—all with their very own Junior Membership card! At just $60, this add-on includes a year of monthly Member Kids’ Club Mornings, among other benefits.

Add to your Membership

Give the gift of Membership

Share transformative art and community with friends and loved ones.

Get started

Frequently asked questions

Other questions about Membership? Museum staff are here to help. Read our extended FAQ, or contact membership@​brooklynmuseum.​org  or 718.501.6326.

When will I receive my Membership card?
How can I access my online account?
How do I apply my Member discount to purchases, both in person and online?
Can I give a Membership as a gift?
Can I designate a secondary Membership cardholder?
